feedback.text: The internet is moving fast, and I do worry about three main things. - cookies - AI - impermanence of media Firstly I would like to see a uniform cookie pop-up. Due to every site having a unique pop-up, one spends real time and effort figuring out where the accept or deny option is. Of course a company would like to steal as much information as possible, so it makes the deny option impossible. Either they make it grey and hidden, or they have you independently select the deny for every partner, which there usually is 1.500 partners. They can also elongate the pop-up, and have you scroll vertically so there can only be 1,5 partner to select at a time. It is time consuming and is draining of energy. The solution should be a simple pop-up, that should be required by law and enforced to be accept, deny or customize. All next to each other, all same size and colour. Secondly, I would like to see real action taken place when it comes to AI. Companies that are based in the EU, should by law indicate when something is AI generated, and should not be able to monetize it. Since generative AI needs alot of training data, alot of companies lobby for easier access to this data. I believe the standard of uploading data to the internet should be protected from data farming. Instead, these companies would have everyone actively opt out of this. Same tactic as cookies, where they overwhelm you, so you just accept defeat. Lastly, I would like to see change in online purchasing. I have many times had an account on Google or somewhere else, where I buy a movie - not rent, but buy. For some reason or another, they lose the rights to the movie, so I can no longer see it in my library. If you buy anything, be it a film, a video game, a picture, there should be an option to still have it after it has been removed from whatever platform. Thank you for reading these comments, I wish you well to whomever is reading this, and I hope that I made my points clear and reasonable.
